If you match the selection criteria and are interested in finding out more about the position, please forward your updated CV to jane.saxby@talentinternational.com by clicking the "APPLY NOW" button. For a list of all vacant positions, please see our website www.talentinternational.com

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues.​ We know work and life are intertwined. That’s why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high-performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.

Your New Role 

This is a 12‑month fixed term opportunity for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join our Member Group within Fund Services.

Reporting to the Business Analysis Team Lead, you will play a critical role in solving complex business problems, identifying improvement opportunities and delivering high‑quality business analysis artefacts that enable successful delivery of digital, software and process change initiatives across the Fund.

You’ll work closely with business stakeholders, delivery teams and partners, contributing both independently and collaboratively to the design and delivery of innovative solutions that support AustralianSuper and our members.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading business analysis activities to support successful delivery of project objectives
Understanding, documenting and improving current and future state business processes
Eliciting, documenting, prioritising and validating high‑quality functional and non‑functional requirements and/or user stories
Facilitating engaging workshops and ensuring clear outcomes and traceability
Supporting business case development, vendor selection and RFP processes where required
Identifying and managing dependencies, risks and issues
Supporting change management and business integration activities
Building strong relationships with stakeholders up to executive level and providing clear, effective communication
Contributing to continuous improvement and capability uplift across the Business Analysis practice

 To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Superannuation experience, essential 
6+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst
2+ years’ experience working within business, digital or IT project environments
Experience working across Agile, Waterfall and hybrid delivery methodologies
Strong capability in producing high‑quality requirements and/or user stories with excellent attention to detail
Proven experience facilitating workshops and engaging diverse stakeholder groups
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence at all levels
Strong problem‑solving skills and the ability to work autonomously while collaborating effectively

Life at AustralianSuper 

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we’re happy to discuss what this looks like for you. 

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity.  We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know. 

What’s Next 

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results. 

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice  

Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.  

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper’s Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

Job Title: Senior Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Analyst
Ad Text:
Company description:



Sandfire continues to make strong progress toward its goal of being a sustainable mining company and global copper producer of significance and is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX: SFR).

Headquartered in Perth, Western Australia, Sandfire has a demonstrated commitment to the highest safety standards. Our strategy is focused on both near-mine and regional opportunities as we seek to leverage our strategic position in the Iberian Pyrite Belt in Spain and the Kalahari Copper Belt in Botswana and materially increase the life of our modern processing hubs.

Sandfire Resources has entered an exciting partnership with Havilah Resources to advance the Kalkaroo Copper-Gold Project in South Australia



Job description:



Sandfire has an opportunity for an experienced Senior Financial Planning and Analysis Analyst with a background in the Mining and Resource industry, to join our talented team of professionals. This role reports to the Group Financial Planning and Analysis manager and will be responsible for leading the accurate and timely delivery of group budgeting and quarterly forecast, preparing analysis and insights to support internal decision-making and market reporting obligations, preparing the group's performance reports and driving continuous improvement to systems, processes and broader FP&A capabilities across the group. This is both strategic and hands-on role, where you will be required to proactively engage and partner with corporate functions in Australia and operations functions globally.





Duties/ Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Manage various stakeholders across the group to ensure accurate and timely delivery of information.
Preparation of budgets, quarterly forecasts, group consolidation of financial plans including the analysis of financial information and production physicals.
Preparation of presentation of planning outputs to support Executive decision-making and approvals.
Maintain and communicate planning timetables in accordance with the Integrated Planning Group Standard and coordination of deliverables by the various business areas.
Preparation of Board papers to support approval of the budget.
Preparation of group performance reporting providing analytical insights to the group's performance against budgets and forecasts.
Preparation of annual and half year reporting deliverables including the operating and financial review sections of these reports.
Provide analysis to support quarterly market reporting, investor updates or other external disclosures.
Maintain a high degree of data integrity across systems, presentations and reports ensuring data is reconciled to source systems and any anomalies are identified and resolved in a timely manner.
Support the transformation of the FP&A function including the design, development and implementation of systems, reports, processes and analysis, as well as support the transition of intra-function responsibilities.
Promote and monitor adherence to Group Standards and Procedures.
Provide business partnering support to corporate functions and operational stakeholders.
Anaplan subject matter expert.
Domestic and international travel as required.

About you

Commerce/Business undergraduate and CA/CPA post graduate qualifications or a technical qualification.
5+ years of experience in FP&A/management accounting/business partnering within the mining sector in a multi-asset company.
ASX-listed company experience highly regarded.
Development and/or management of complex financial models
Ability to work to pre-specified deadlines without rework.
Ability to work with global stakeholders from different cultural backgrounds.
System experience with major ERPs and planning systems (Anaplan, OneStream and Pronto highly desirable).
Solid understanding of accounting principles, financial statements and production and sales measures applicable to the mining industry.
Excellent analytical and numerical skills, with the proven ability to synthesise large data sets with a high attention to detail.
Project and time management across multiple tasks (challenging, complex and dynamic deliverables and tight timelines).
Advanced Excel skills.
Autonomous and self-starter that can work independently
Good leadership qualities to actively mentor and support team members and other stakeholders.

Brunel is partnered with a leading resources organisation on a contract opportunity for a Data Analyst. This role is well suited to someone with experinece across Databricks SQL workflows and delivering Power BI reporting in a mining environment.

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Investment Operations and Performance team as a Senior Investment Performance Specialist.  If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane or Sydney, we’d love you to apply. 

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As a Senior Investment Performance Specialist, you will be accountable for delivering high quality performance measurement and attribution insights, configuring and enhancing system-based reporting (including PEARL) and strengthening sustainable BAU processes through automation and migration away from Excel based models.




Day to day, you'll:

Own end-to-end performance & attribution reporting across investment managers, asset classes and options—delivering accurate, scalable, well-controlled outputs with clear interpretation aligned to governance and senior stakeholder expectations.

Drive continuous improvement—identify and progress opportunities for automation, efficiency, stronger controls and process enhancements informed by industry best practice.

Partner closely with Investment teams and stakeholders to gather requirements, clarify objectives, and convert investment needs into fit-for-purpose reporting and analytics.

Deliver system and automation uplift for BAU reporting (e.g., PEARL configuration/enhancements, testing and parallel runs), reducing reliance on Excel and leveraging SQL/analytics tools to streamline data and processes.

Provide SME leadership and 2IC support—peer review, coaching, escalation for complex BAU matters, and coordination of competing priorities to ensure critical reporting deadlines are met.

This role is available as a permanent opportunity. 

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Strong knowledge of performance measurement and attribution methodologies (e.g. Brinson-Fachler) and stakeholder-focused interpretation.
Strong experience in investment performance measurement and attribution, either with an investment manager, asset owner, or a custodian.
Experience analysing multi-asset portfolios and investment structures.
Experience with data and process controls for performance and attribution (reconciliations, validation and documentation).
CFA or CIPM (or working towards) is desirable.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Thursday, 14 April 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

Senior Business Analyst - Permanent Full Time - Perth CBD



The Senior Business Analyst provides expert advisory and analytical support to business leaders, translating strategy into clearly defined, value‑driven business initiatives. Acting as a trusted subject matter expert, the role leads complex analysis, gap assessments, and stakeholder workshops to shape sustainable business outcomes. It bridges strategic planning and delivery by defining high‑level solutions, success measures, and initiative viability. Through strong engagement with senior stakeholders, the role enables informed, data‑driven decision making aligned to organisational objectives and whole‑of‑business value.

Your Role in Action

Translate government policy, regulatory drivers and system planning outcomes into clear business requirements, priority initiatives and delivery pathways.
Support the development and refinement of Priority Project determinations, investment justifications and strategic option assessments.
Work closely with Network Planning, Asset Management, Project Development, Delivery, Regulation and Energy Policy teams to ensure initiatives are coherent, integrated and appropriately sequenced.
Analyse and challenge proposed scope, staging and timing to ensure alignment with:
Strategic transmission objectives
System security and reliability requirements
Deliverability and workforce constraints
Funding and regulatory pathways
Facilitate targeted workshops, planning sessions and analysis to identify gaps, dependencies and opportunities for improvement.
Provide a strong ‘whole‑of‑business' lens, proactively identifying risks, misalignments and trade‑offs — and raising them early with practical options.
Contribute to high‑quality materials for senior leadership, Board and Government engagement, including briefings, decision papers and assurance artefacts.
Act as a trusted adviser to the CEL Development Manager, providing clear, evidence‑based advice in situations of ambiguity, competing priorities and time pressure.
Performing NPV, NPC, IRR, and sensitivity analysis
Quantifying benefits (financial, economic, reliability, emissions, risk reduction) and Testing assumptions (discount rates, asset lives, demand scenarios)

What Makes You a Great Fit

Formal study, development or qualifications in business, finance, business analysis, risk and / or data analytics / modelling.
Extensive experience working on or supporting large, regulated infrastructure programs — ideally in electricity transmission, utilities, transport or similarly complex environments.
Strong understanding of capital investment lifecycles, including early‑stage option development, business case preparation and portfolio prioritisation.
Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in high‑ambiguity environments where scope, policy settings and delivery pathways continue to evolve.
Experience engaging with, or supporting work involving, government agencies, regulators or system planners (e.g. Energy Policy WA, AEMO, ERA or equivalent).
High levels of commercial and strategic judgement, with the confidence to constructively challenge technical, planning and delivery teams.
Proven ability to communicate complex issues clearly and calmly to senior, time‑poor stakeholders.
Strong relationship‑building capability across multidisciplinary teams where priorities and incentives may differ.
Formal qualifications or professional development in business, finance, business analysis, risk or data analytics.

Tools & Technology

Power BI
Office Timeline or similar visual planning tools
High proficiency across Microsoft Office products

Performance Drivers is seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support a major project within a large and complex public sector environment.

This role will work across asset management, asset data, capital planning, business process improvement and stakeholder engagement. The successful candidate will help translate business needs into clear requirements, process documentation and implementation-ready outputs that support improved asset management capability.




About the Role

The Senior Business Analyst will work closely with project leads, subject matter experts, operational stakeholders and system users to understand current-state processes, define future-state requirements and support the delivery of improved asset management practices.

The role requires someone who can operate in a complex stakeholder environment, bring structure to unclear requirements, and produce practical documentation that supports system, process and capability uplift.

Key Responsibilities

Engage stakeholders across asset management, capital planning, operations, finance, procurement and technology teams.

Capture current-state processes, pain points, data flows, business rules and system dependencies.

Who are we looking for?


We are seeking a Market Research and Insights Manager to join Marketing and Student Recruitment, Engagement, Chancellery Division.

The Research and Insights Manager provides strategic leadership for research, analytics and insight across Marketing & Student Recruitment, supporting the Chief Marketing Officer and senior leaders with evidence-based advice that improves marketing effectiveness, recruitment performance and the student experience. The role develops and executes an integrated research and analytics roadmap, establishes robust governance and quality practices for student-related insight, and translates complex data into clear, decision-ready recommendations. The role owns brand tracking and reputation research for Marketing & Student Recruitment, working in partnership with other areas of the University to ensure brand, market and student insights are aligned, shared and effectively applied. This is a senior role requiring strong influence, strategic thinking and the ability to connect insights to action in a complex stakeholder environment. The role works closely with the Data and Analytics function in Digital Business Solutions, among other internal connections. This position reports to the Chief Marketing Officer for career planning, development, and performance expectations.

We’re offering this role as a hybrid position as part of QUT’s commitment to embracing the opportunities created by a more virtual and connected world.



About us

QUT is a major Australian university with a global outlook and a 'real world' focus. We are an ambitious and collaborative institution that seeks to equip our students and graduates with the skills they will need in an increasingly disrupted and challenged world.

Marketing and Student Recruitment is responsible for building and protecting QUT’s brand and reputation and engaging and recruiting future students. As well as professional services provided to the university community, the Department is responsible for a variety of communication functions and oversees relevant policies, protocols and governance arrangements.



What you need to succeed

1. Education, training and/or experience equivalent to postgraduate qualifications with extensive relevant experience in higher education, student recruitment, or similarly complex customer led sectors (e.g. finance, health, digital subscriptions, government services).
2. Extensive analytics and research experience, with a strong record of leading teams or influencing across complex organisations, supported by demonstrated capability in coaching, performance management, team development and continuous improvement.
3. Skilled in building strong stakeholder partnerships, balancing competing priorities and driving uptake of insights, with a proven ability to shape strategy and influence executive decisions through clear, evidence based recommendations.
4. Strong expertise in research design and delivery across qualitative and quantitative methods, including survey and panel based research, and experience design approaches (UX and/or service design).
5. Advanced analytics capability including interpreting and communicating performance insights using data visualisation tools and applying experimentation frameworks, forecasting and predictive modelling in business contexts, and working knowledge of digital analytics and performance measurement approaches (e.g. GA4, attribution, tag management).
6. Strong understanding of data governance and quality practices including privacy, ethical use, and trusted metric definition.
7. Experience working with Salesforce CRM in a complex environment, including integration with marketing automation, digital analytics and BI tools, alongside familiarity with cloud data platforms and modern data practices (e.g. Azure, AWS, Snowflake).
8. Demonstrated commitment to equity, diversity and Indigenous Australian engagement.

We're committed to building a culture that fosters connection between people and purpose. Beyond personal and professional fulfillment, a career at QUT offers:

A healthy work-life balance with a blend of on-campus and off-campus work arrangements for applicable positions. 
Competitive remuneration with up to 17% superannuation and leave loading of 17.5%. 
Generous parental leave including primary carer parental leave of up to 26 weeks (including 17% super).

Salary Packaging Scheme for additional superannuation contributions, vehicle leasing, and on-campus services such as childcare, parking, and gyms. 
Numerous opportunities for professional development including leadership programs and workshops, and our study assistance scheme.
Commitment to our Indigenous Australian staff  through initiatives such as the Indigenous Australian Staff Network, Cultural and Ceremonial Leave, Staff Development Workshops, and representation on university committees.

We are guided by our values of Ambition, Integrity, Inclusiveness, Innovation, and Academic Freedom and our QUT Connections in our mission to inspire and shape the next generation of change-makers.

About Us – Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR), NSW Health
Be part of the team leading the largest digital transformation in Australia’s public health history. NSW Health’s SDPR program is transforming the way patient care is delivered and experienced - creating a single, secure, holistic, and integrated view of patient information across the entire state.

 

By connecting every corner of the NSW public health system, this groundbreaking program empowers clinicians and care teams with real-time access to unified patient data - enabling safer, more transparent, and quality care for every person, anywhere.  
 

Join us now and contribute to the millions of ways we're enriching health every day.
 

About the Opportunity
As a Senior Integration Analyst you will lead the development, maintenance and support of a complex integration framework that underpins the Single Digital Patient Record (SDPR) program. This role is pivotal in enabling seamless data exchange across NSW Health systems to improve patient care and clinical outcomes.
 

You’ll work across technical and clinical domains, providing subject matter expertise and strategic guidance to ensure integration solutions are robust, secure and aligned with evolving program needs. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to a state-wide digital transformation initiative that impacts healthcare delivery at scale.

 

In this role, you will:

Lead integration efforts across NSW Health, ensuring alignment with program architecture and strategic objectives.
Collaborate with stakeholders to design, test and implement application interfaces and technical workflows.
Provide expert advice on integration risks, upgrades and architectural changes to maintain system integrity.
Coordinate and validate system integration testing, ensuring quality and reliability of data exchange.
Resolve complex incidents impacting integration architecture and communicate effectively with stakeholders.

 

About You
You are a strategic and technically skilled integration professional who thrives in complex environments and enjoys solving challenges that impact healthcare delivery at scale. You bring:

Extensive experience in developing, maintaining and supporting integration frameworks within large, complex organisations
Strong leadership in system integration testing, interface validation and technical workflow design
Proven ability to collaborate with diverse stakeholders, including clinical, technical and executive teams
Expertise in identifying and mitigating integration risks, and advising on upgrades and architectural changes
Advanced problem-solving skills with a focus on service-level alignment and continuous improvement
Proficiency in documenting integration requirements, data models and process flows
A commitment to delivering secure, reliable and scalable integration solutions that support digital health transformation.

 

You will succeed in this role if you:

Enjoy working in an environment where no two days are the same. 
Are motivated by tight deadlines and problem solving on the go. 
Can juggle multiple priorities while staying calm and outcome focused. 
Thrive when collaborating with others to deliver results in a fast-moving space.

Ad Text:
The Payments Settlements (PS) department is seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join a dynamic team responsible for delivering security initiatives and major projects that maintain and enhance Australia's critical payment systems. These include the Reserve Bank Information and Transfer System (RITS), the Fast Settlement Service (FSS), and SWIFT systems used for interbank payments. In this role, you will act as a key liaison between stakeholders to elicit, analyse, communicate, and validate requirements for changes that strengthen the security and resilience of PS systems.


Your role

Develop detailed business requirements and functional specifications for the new business initiatives and improvements for RITS and other critical systems operated by PS.
Collaborate with business and IT stakeholders to capture and document business and technical requirements and act as a liaison between business, IT, and infrastructure teams to align solutions with business goals.
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder engagement sessions to drive consensus and clarity
Support solution design and architecture discussions with technical teams
Work with Security, Risk, and Compliance teams to address security vulnerabilities and deliver various initiatives to uplift security of PS systems and ensuring compliance with regulatory, security, and technology assurance frameworks.
Maintain requirements traceability matrix, conduct risk assessments, and manage impact on business processes.
Create and maintain confluence pages, Jira user stories, acceptance criteria, and backlog refinement for the initiatives/ projects assigned.
Lead end-to-end business analysis activities across multiple initiatives/ projects
Support knowledge management initiatives across the department by documenting & consolidating the functional specifications, business processes, standard operating procedures and operations manuals which were implemented through various projects over a period.
Assist with project documentation, administration, and governance


Your team

The Security and Resilience Analysis (SRA) team is part of the Payments Settlements Business Delivery section. We work collaboratively with stakeholders to deliver projects that strengthen and enhance Australia's payments system. Our team leads the planning, management, and implementation of security and resilience initiatives across the department, including the SWIFT Customer Security Programme attestation. Safe, secure and reliable settlement systems are integral to the smooth functioning of the Australian economy, and projects relating to these systems are a critical aspect of the RBA's work.


Your background

To be successful in this critical role you will possess:


Advanced knowledge of business analysis practices, tools, and techniques.
Experience in the specification and testing arrangements required for mission critical systems.
Business Analysis experience in IT infrastructure and/or security assessment related projects.
Understanding of payment and settlement system operations in Australia.
Knowledge of the security and resilience aspects required for the development and operation of critical systems will be beneficial though not mandatory.
The ability to communicate complex technical issues to both business and technical audiences.
Strong analytical skills, written & verbal communication, and stakeholder engagement capabilities with excellent negotiation skills.
Skill in creating clear and impactful presentations for workshops, training, and create impactful technical/business documentation.
Ability to learn quickly in unfamiliar and complex environments with keen interest in understanding new technologies, business processes and solving problems.
Strong experience in Requirements Management, with the ability to independently manage BA activities and deliverables
Experience producing project artefacts such as business cases, project plans, RACI matrices, status reporting, managing project risk and issues, and exposure to procurement processes.
Professional certification in business analysis (e.g. IIBA CBAP) would be highly regarded.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

About MSA National

MSA National is an Australian legal and technology company and the country’s largest outsource provider of mortgage documentation and settlement services to major financial institutions. Operating from offices in Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane, we handle high volumes of settlements daily and continue to grow. Our people, our systems and our service standards are the foundation of everything we do.


The Opportunity

AI represents the most significant strategic opportunity in front of our business this decade. We are establishing a dedicated AI function, sitting outside of technology and reporting directly to the Executive, to drive this agenda with the rigour and focus it deserves.

As Head of AI & Analytics, you will own the strategy, the use-case portfolio, the adoption metrics and the executive-level narrative. You will also hold accountability for our data and analytics foundations — the infrastructure that underpins effective AI deployment. This is a strategic and commercial leadership role: we have engineering capability for the build; we need you for the thinking, the prioritisation and the orchestration.

You will work alongside our AI Change Lead, who manages the people side of the programme, and report directly to the Executive, providing regular reporting on where adoption is progressing, where value is being realised and where the business should focus next.


What You'll Be Doing

•       Own the AI strategy for MSA National — determining what we build, what we procure, what we partner on, and in what sequence

•       Lead the AI function: surface use cases from across the business, prioritise them against value and risk, and manage them from concept through to production

•       Maintain governance oversight of all AI tools and automation in use across the business, ensuring visibility, monitoring and accountability

•       Own our data warehouse, business intelligence reporting and analytics roadmap; ensure our AI programme has the data foundations it requires

•       Partner with the AI Change Lead on staff training, AI tool rollout and internal capability-building

•       Partner with IT, Risk and the CISO on deployment governance, risk controls and responsible AI practices

•       Evaluate and run proof-of-concept engagements with AI vendors and partners, bringing commercial rigour to selection decisions

•       Present regular AI programme reporting to the Executive team, covering adoption, use cases in progress, value delivered and emerging risks

•       Monitor the external AI landscape and provide the Executive with timely intelligence on developments relevant to our business and sector





What You'll Bring

Essential

•       7+ years in a senior analytics, data or AI leadership role, ideally within a mid-sized financial services, legal services or shared-services business

•       A genuinely strategic mindset — experienced in portfolio prioritisation and connecting AI investment to measurable business outcomes

•       Deep AI fluency — able to engage credibly with engineers on predictive, generative and agentic AI, while operating primarily as a strategist and orchestrator rather than a practitioner

•       Strong commercial instincts — you connect every use case back to cost-to-serve, revenue, speed or customer experience

•       Hands-on familiarity with data infrastructure — data warehouses, BI platforms and a sound understanding of data quality requirements

•       Exceptional communication skills — equally effective briefing an executive team, authoring a policy document and engaging a frontline team

•       Experience managing external vendor and partner relationships, including proof-of-concept to production processes


Desirable:

•       Hands-on experience building and deploying AI agents or automation tools

•       Background in mortgage, conveyancing, legal or PEXA-adjacent businesses

•       Completion of a structured AI leadership programme

•       Prior experience establishing an AI or analytics function from the ground up




 What We Offer

•        A senior leadership role with direct Executive sponsorship and broad organisational visibility

•       The opportunity to design and build the AI function from first principles, rather than inherit an existing one

•       An Executive team that is genuinely committed to the AI agenda and actively engaged in its success

•       Modern Macquarie Park offices and the scale of a national business

•       Competitive base salary and short-term incentive, a professional development budget, and a clear mandate to reshape how we operate


How to Apply

Please apply via SEEK with your CV and a brief cover note. We would welcome your reflections on one AI or analytics initiative you are particularly proud of, and how you measured its impact.

Shortlisted candidates will meet the CEO, then our Head of Operations and CIO, then a small panel from the Executive team. We aim to have the successful candidate in the role within 30 to 45 days.


General Guidelines

All enquiries will be treated in the strictest confidence. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. This is strictly a full-time, work from office position.

Job Title: Vice President, Fixed Asset Advisory Services
Ad Text:
In a world of disruption and increasingly complex business challenges, our professionals bring truth into focus with the Kroll Lens. Our sharp analytical skills, paired with the latest technology, allow us to give our clients clarity—not just answers— in all areas of business. We embrace diverse backgrounds and global perspectives, and we cultivate diversity by respecting, including, and valuing one another. As part of One team, One Kroll, you’ll contribute to a supportive and collaborative work environment that empowers you to excel. 

Our Fixed Asset Advisory Services (FAAS) practice provides fixed asset valuations to a diverse portfolio of global clients.

At Kroll, your work will help deliver clarity to our clients’ most complex governance, risk, and transparency challenges. Apply now to join One team, One Kroll. 

RESPONSIBILITIES:

1. Global methodology & cost alignment

Drive alignment and standardisation of valuation and cost modelling methodologies across multiple geographies, business units and engagement types.

Reconcile and consolidate disparate cost-related data sources that exist across the business, establishing a single, coherent framework for cost benchmarking that aligns with global requirements.


2. Tech-enabled process automation 

Lead the design, development and deployment of AI-enabled tools that automate and accelerate the valuation process modelling lifecycle, from data ingestion and cost estimation through to final reporting.

Work cross-functionally with Kroll's technology and data teams (with technical support available) to specify requirements, test prototypes and scale proven tools across the practice.

Leverage generative AI, machine learning and advanced analytics to embed intelligence into valuation workflows, reducing manual effort and enhancing analytical rigour.

Champion a culture of digital innovation within the Fixed Assets practice, identifying opportunities where technology can materially improve quality, consistency and profitability of engagements.


3. Data Strategy & Structured Dataset Development

Unlock the value of Kroll's extensive historical engagement dataset by leading the conversion of unstructured asset data — accumulated across prior mandates globally — into clean, structured and queryable datasets.

Integrate structured datasets into active valuation engagements to improve cost benchmarking, market-rate assumptions and analytical depth, reducing reliance on external data sources.

Identify patterns, trends and insights from aggregated engagement data to support knowledge development, proposal writing and thought leadership.


4. People leadership & capability building

Upskilling team members by driving tech-enablement across the business 

REQUIREMENTS:

Significant experience in valuation, fixed assets, cost modelling or a related advisory discipline.

Demonstrated track record of leading process improvement, automation or data‑led transformation initiatives.

Strong commercial mindset with the ability to link technology initiatives to margin, efficiency and scalability outcomes.

Experience working across regions and influencing senior stakeholders without direct authority.

Comfort working alongside technology teams, with the ability to translate business requirements into practical, scalable solutions.

Recognized technical expertise in valuation or within a specific industry vertical; ability to communicate complex technical issues to senior executives.

Strong people leadership skills with a track record of coaching, team development, and fostering collaborative culture.

High level of integrity and professionalism

Commitment and aptitude to grow our business and brand

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with maturity and confidence presenting to senior stakeholders.

About Kroll 

Join the global leader in risk and financial advisory solutions—Kroll. With a nearly century-long legacy, we blend trusted expertise with cutting-edge technology to navigate and redefine industry complexities. As a part of One Team, One Kroll, you'll contribute to a collaborative and empowering environment, propelling your career to new heights. Ready to build, protect, restore and maximize our clients’ value? Your journey begins with Kroll. 

Kroll is committed to equal opportunity and diversity, and recruits people based on merit. 

In order to be considered for a position, you must formally apply via careers.kroll.com

Ad Text:
The role 

Reporting to the Director, Service Management and improvement, the Reporting and Analysis Manager is responsible for delivering customer insights to eHealth Queensland staff and identifying performance insights that promote end-to-end improvement in eHealth Queensland's service delivery to its customers.

The Reporting and Analysis Manager leads a specialist team of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting experts, managing and setting the direction of current and future activities. The manager adds value through extensive knowledge of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting, coupled with extensive knowledge of the operations of eHealth Queensland and its customers, to implement value-adding performance reporting and analysis solutions based on industry standards and best practices. They are expected to possess leadership skills to bring together multi-disciplined teams to embed performance reporting and analysis into service operations as well as providing peer mentoring, guidance and internal development. The role will work broadly across the Enterprise Technology Services branch plus other areas of eHealth, with varied stakeholders and customers.

Key responsibilities:

Manage the core eHealth Queensland customer intelligence and service performance reporting and analysis operational processes ensuring that they meet the needs of eHealth Queensland's customers and support teams
Measure and report outcomes and performance against defined metrics
Optimise toolsets to standardise and automate processes as much as possible
Lead and manage a team of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting specialists, instilling a culture of customer-first, continual improvement and analytical best practice and innovation
Develop and maintain the framework for service delivery performance reporting and analysis, ensuring that the process provides trustworthy data and timely delivery of information and analysis to reduce the delays in decision-making processes caused by complex or disparate manual processes, and provides end-to-end insight across eHealth Queensland's services

About you

We are searching for someone who:

Demonstrated knowledge and experience of the service management capabilities of ServiceNow, the SQL Server toolset as it supports best practice data management, specifically in a data warehousing environment, and Power BI to provide information that supports effective decision-making.
Demonstrated high level understanding of Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting frameworks and best practice, as they apply to a large ICT customer service delivery organisation
Demonstrated ability to design, implement and promote effective Business Intelligence and Performance Reporting frameworks and solutions within a large, complex service delivery organisation
Advanced interpersonal, communication, negotiation, consultation and collaboration skills incorporating the ability to liaise and consult effectively with personnel at all levels within and external to eHealth Queensland
Demonstrated high level organisational skills including the ability to establish own work priorities and manage time effectively, whilst remaining flexible to respond to changing needs in a busy environment
High level oral and written communication skills, including the ability to compile complex reports, briefs, correspondence and submissions on behalf of the Director, Service Management and Improvement
Demonstrated high level research, analytical, conceptual and problem solving skills including the proven capacity to initiate appropriate strategic and operational responses to key issues affecting the enterprise
Highly developed stakeholder engagement and management skills with the ability to build strong relationships with both internal and external stakeholders
Demonstrated ability to lead, manage and mentor staff committed to the delivery of a diverse and dynamic work program to achieve positive service outcomes for customers while effectively managing conflicting demands and tight time frames
